257: 
258:     /// Reparent direct children to a new parent without changing `parent_head`.
259:     ///
260:     /// `parent_head` tracks the commit the child is currently based on. Reparenting
261:     /// alone does not move the branch tip, so that old base must be preserved until
262:     /// a later restack/rebase actually happens.
263:     pub fn reparent_children_preserving_parent_head(
264:         &mut self,
265:         old_parent: &str,
266:         new_parent: &str,
267:     ) -> Result<Vec<String>> {
268:         let children = self.children_of(old_parent);
269:         for child_name in &children {
270:             let child = self.get_branch_mut(child_name)?;
271:             child.parent = new_parent.to_string();
272:         }
273:         Ok(children)
274:     }
275: 
276:     pub fn is_trunk(&self, branch: &str) -> bool {
277:         branch == self.trunk
278:     }
279: 
280:     pub fn is_managed(&self, branch: &str) -> bool {
281:         self.branches.contains_key(branch)
282:     }
283: 
284:     /// Returns branches in topological order (parents before children).
285:     pub fn topo_order(&self) -> Vec<String> {
286:         let mut result = Vec::new();
287:         let mut visited = std::collections::HashSet::new();
288: 
289:         fn visit(
290:             name: &str,
291:             state: &StackState,
292:             visited: &mut std::collections::HashSet<String>,
293:             result: &mut Vec<String>,
294:         ) {
295:             if visited.contains(name) || state.is_trunk(name) {
296:                 return;
297:             }
298:             visited.insert(name.to_string());
299:             if let Some(meta) = state.branches.get(name) {
300:                 visit(&meta.parent, state, visited, result);
301:             }
302:             result.push(name.to_string());
303:         }
